projects.name: Hierarch
projects.description: React task management app with drag-and-drop, focus timer, and today view
projects.notes: The Today view is replaced by a designer's Briefing — a two-column morning dashboard with active projects (2/3 width), recent progress timeline (1/3), needs-attention items, and a design notes capture area with task linking and convert-to-task. The focus timer is removed from the sidebar and integrated into task rows as an inline icon that shows a live pulsing countdown when running, with a redesigned full-screen timer view featuring a progress ring, large play/pause button, and session notepad that appends to the task on save. Resources view is now a table list with checkboxes and bulk actions. Next: Insights view for phase analytics, deploy these changes, and continue the design cockpit vision.

projects.name: Kinlo
projects.description: iOS health tracking app for desk workers — workouts, water intake, movement streaks

projects.name: RockPass
projects.notes: Full Phase 1 scaffold is complete and running in Xcode on iPhone 16 Pro simulator. Built 5 screens (Home, Gyms, My Pass, Gear, Account) in Expo Router with a custom elevated center tab bar, Google Maps integration with a live API key restricted to com.rockpass.app, and a 909-line Figma plugin ready to generate all screens in the RockPass Figma file. Next: run the Figma plugin, wire up real gym data via Google Places API, and build the auth layer.

projects.name: hierarch-fos
projects.notes: Hierarch FOS is live at tryhierarch.vercel.app — a Linear-style marketing site with a sandy/fabric color palette, real app UI mocks, and the actual Hierarch logo throughout. Mobile responsive with CSS media queries covering nav, hero mock, feature grid, footer, and CTA. Next: wire up real waitlist/signup, add a pricing section, and connect hierarchical.app domain.

projects.name: mission-control
projects.description: Claude Code command center — live dashboard tracking projects, skills, MCPs, and session history
projects.notes: Mission Control is fully deployed on Vercel with a live project status card view — each project in ~/Apps gets a card showing its stack, status, and a Claude-written status note sorted by most recent activity. The End Mission flow is wired end-to-end: saying it updates the project's .mc.json, syncs to GitHub, and the hosted dashboard reflects it within seconds.

projects.name: portfolio
projects.notes: All three A/B test case studies (Mobile Web Experience, Mobile Email Editor, Contacts Upload) are fully built with accurate experiment data, comparison bar charts, and detailed narratives. Charts and metric cards dynamically adapt to dark/light mode via CSS variables. A page-level lightbox with prev/next navigation is wired up for all project images. Email editor page now focuses on MEQ-depth breakdowns for both sending and creation. Markdown exports of all three experiments are in casestudies/ for cross-project reference. Next: continue polish or add new case studies.

projects.name: showcase
projects.notes: Showcase is live at showcase.aaroncougle.com with 4 slides using a clean flex-based bento layout — 2/3 hero on the left, stacked thumbnails on the right on desktop. Mobile stacks them vertically with a 250px hero on top and a horizontal thumbnail row below. The site is fully deployed and polished; no pending tasks.

projects.name: website-analyzer
projects.description: Next.js tool for analyzing and auditing websites
